description:
## Component: hx-time-picker (NEW)
**Tier 2 — Standard Enterprise | Industry Consensus: 2/6 libraries | HEALTHCARE-CRITICAL**

Time input with optional dropdown for common time slots.

### ADR-001 Classification: Hybrid (Form Component)

### API Design
**Properties:** `name`, `value` (HH:MM format), `min`, `max`, `step` (minutes), `label`, `required`, `disabled`, `error`, `format` (12h|24h)
**Slots:** Default (label), `help`, `error`
**CSS Parts:** `input`, `listbox`, `option`, `label`
**Events:** `hx-change` (composed: true)

### Drupal Integration
```twig
<hx-time-picker name="appointment_time" label="Appointment Time" min="08:00" max="17:00" step="15" format="12h">
</hx-time-picker>
```

### ElementInternals: Submits HH:MM time string
### Healthcare: Appointment times, medication schedules, shift scheduling

### Acceptance Criteria
1. Dropdown with time slots at step intervals
2. Text input with time format masking
3. 12-hour and 24-hour formats
4. Min/max time constraints
5. ElementInternals form participation
title: T2-33: hx-time-picker — Time Input Field

coderabbitai: **Actionable comments posted: 7**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-time-picker/hx-time-picker.stories.ts`:
- Around line 177-713: Replace all non-null assertions and unsafe type
assertions inside the Story play helpers (e.g., uses of querySelector(...)!,
host.shadowRoot!, and "as unknown as HTMLElement" in plays such as
AppointmentScheduler.play, WithError.play, EventVerification.play,
KeyboardNavigation.play, FocusManagement.play, etc.) with safe guards: call
querySelector/querySelectorAll and getNativeInput into properly typed variables,
check for null/undefined and either throw a clear Error or early-return the play
(so tests fail clearly), and narrow types via runtime checks (e.g., if (!host)
throw new Error('hx-time-picker not found'); const shadow = host.shadowRoot; if
(!shadow) throw new Error('shadowRoot missing');). Update any uses of
within(...) to accept the narrowed HTMLElement type after checks. Ensure no "!"
or "as unknown as" remain and that getNativeInput is used only after verifying
the canvas contains the expected element.

In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-time-picker/hx-time-picker.styles.ts`:
- Around line 10-230: The stylesheet uses hardcoded literal fallbacks (hex
colors like `#343a40`, spacing like 0.25rem, timing like 150ms, numeric z-index,
etc.) which violates the token-only theming rule; update every literal fallback
to use the corresponding --hx-* token-only fallback instead (e.g., replace color
fallbacks in .field__label, .field__required-marker, .field__combobox,
.field__combobox:focus-within, .field--error rules, .field__toggle,
.field__listbox, .field__option, .field__option--selected,
.field__input::placeholder, .field__help-text, .field__error), swap spacing and
size literals in .field, .field__input, .field__toggle, padding/gap

threadFeedback.agentReasoning: Line 712 uses `host.shadowRoot!.activeElement` — a non-null assertion banned by project standards ("No non-null assertions"). While `shadowRoot` is always present on a Lit element, the `!` violates the zero-tolerance policy. Fix is trivial.

Line 230 hardcodes `#dc3545` as a fallback: `var(--hx-time-picker-error-color, var(--hx-color-error-500, #dc3545))`. Project non-negotiable: "No hardcoded values — Colors, spacing, typography, and timing use design tokens. Always." The final fallback must be removed — semantic tokens are the floor.

All six imports at line 1-2 (`fixture`, `shadowQuery`, `shadowQueryAll`, `oneEvent`, `cleanup`, `checkA11y`) are verified exported from test-utils.ts. The `beforeEach` vitest import is used in tests across the 85-test suite. Tests pass — no genuine import issue exists at this location.

CRITICAL: `private _inputEl!: HTMLInputElement` uses a definite assignment assertion (`!`), which is explicitly banned by project standards ("No non-null assertions"). The `@query` decorator returns `null` when the element is not found — the `!` suppresses that type safety. Downstream, `_updateValidity()` passes `this._inputEl` to `setValidity()` without null-guarding, which could cause a runtime error if called before first render.

`this._internals.setFormValue(this.value)` at line 316 passes `''` (empty string) when the field is cleared. An empty string and `null` have different form-submission semantics — `null` means "no value submitted" while `''` submits an empty field. For an unset required time picker, the form value should be `null`.

`e.preventDefault()` is called unconditionally in the `Enter` case, even when `_open` is false. This blocks form submission whenever the time picker input has focus and the user presses Enter with the dropdown closed — a real behavioral bug. Prevention should only occur when actually handling the dropdown (selecting an item).
